behaviors_covered: BlockHashWithGroupId 4-byte big-endian packing (K08); round-trip get_block_hash/get_group_id
hash_block_tokens determinism (same inputs → same hash) and collision-resistance (different parent/body/extras → different hash)
extra_keys (K09) folded into every chain hash — same tokens with lora/mm/cache_salt extras must NOT collide
chain_block_hashes: only FULL blocks hashed; trailing partial dropped (Trap 2)
K03 chain property: changing token i in block k shifts H_k AND every H_j for j > k
common_prefix_length: stops at first divergence; empty inputs handled
BlockHashToBlockMap 3-branch insert (empty/int/dict) — K02 no-dedup invariant
pop: int-branch match removes; mismatch restores; dict-branch removes specific block; missing key returns None
get_cached_block (K07): single-group hit/miss; multi-group all-must-hit; one-group-misses returns None
Trie/RadixTree pedagogical baselines: insert+find_longest_prefix, edge splitting, mid-edge divergence break, prefix-then-extension
PrefixCacheManager.find_longest_cache_hit: empty → []; full match returns all blocks; K03 partial match stops at first miss; max_length caps lookup window
cache_blocks: full-block-only (Trap 2); idempotent on re-call; growth caches only new suffix
evict_block: known block succeeds, unknown returns False
Chain-break invariant: evict block 0 → all subsequent miss (the prefix-cache fundamental)
Eviction-of-middle-block: block 0 still hits; block 1 misses → stops there (verified by find_longest_cache_hit semantics)
touch/register_request bumps ref_cnt; free_request decrements but K10 cache entries persist
prefix_aware_allocate: first request 0 hits; second reuses prefix; disjoint 0 hits; partial block not cached
get_computed_blocks: K04 max_cache_hit_length = num_tokens - 1 (logits gap); first request empty result; second returns prefix
allocate_with_prefix_cache: full demo §3 trace (A:0 hits, B:2 hits, C:0 hits, D:0 after eviction chain break)
Demo §4 78% KV savings exactly: naive=2000, prefix-aware=432, saved=1568, ratio=0.784
num_blocks_saved formula: (num_reqs - 1) * sys_prompt_blocks scaling verified across (10, 4), (20, 8), (50, 32)
verify_invariants: I1, I2, I3 all PASS on demo workload AND empty manager
Hit-rate sweep: 0.10/0.50/0.90 prefix shares yield expected hit rates within ±2.5% of demo
Hash-vs-radix speedup REAL not synthetic: in test runs, hash table beats both Trie and RadixTree on per-block lookup
Cross-chapter regression: Ch04 scheduler.py + Ch05 block_pool.py + Ch06 request_queue.py all importable alongside Ch07

demo_reproducibility.section2_hash_speedup_demo: 4.65x vs Trie, ~4.83x vs RadixTree (demo run @ N=1000 prefixes × 5000 iter)
demo_reproducibility.section2_hash_speedup_test: verified hash < both, no strict ratio (CI-stable)
demo_reproducibility.section3_chain_break_demo: D allocates 4 blocks, hit=0 after evicting block 0
demo_reproducibility.section4_kv_savings_test: exact match on all four numbers
demo_reproducibility.section5_invariants_demo: I1 PASS, I2 PASS, I3 PASS

hash_vs_radix_4_6x_real.method: test_hash_table_faster_than_trie + test_hash_table_faster_than_radix_tree run live timing comparison
hash_vs_radix_4_6x_real.demo_evidence: 60-second demo run shows Trie 80291/s, RadixTree 77232/s, Hash 373348/s — 4.65x speedup
hash_vs_radix_4_6x_real.explanation: Hash table = single dict lookup per chained block hash (Python C-level dict). Trie/RadixTree walk Python-level pointers per token, paying O(L) interpreter overhead. The chain hash provides 'tree property' WITHOUT tree traversal cost.
hash_vs_radix_4_6x_real.synthetic_or_real: REAL — different algorithms, different complexity profiles, measured over millions of operations

chain_break_correctness.the_invariant: find_longest_cache_hit returns block_ids[0..k] where k is the FIRST index whose chain hash misses. Evicting any block i causes all blocks j>=i to be unreachable via chain lookup.

hash_collision_resistance.evidence: sha256-based hash; same body with different parent or extras yields different output deterministically

78pct_kv_savings_arithmetic.derivation: naive = N * (sys + user) / B; prefix-aware = sys + N * user / B (first req pays full sys; rest share). saved = (N-1) * sys / B. For N=50, sys=32 blocks, user=8 blocks: saved = 49*32 = 1568, ratio = 1568/2000 = 0.784
78pct_kv_savings_arithmetic.claim_validity: EXACT; (num_reqs - 1) * sys_prompt_blocks scaling verified at 3 sweep points

wisdom_applied: W02 'don't pass for the wrong reason' applied via exact-number assertions: chain-break test would fail loudly if find_longest_cache_hit ever returned hit>=1 after block 0 eviction; KV savings test pins the exact 1568 figure not just 'saved>0'.
knowledge_applied: K01 (hash table not radix tree): test_hash_table_faster_than_trie + test_hash_table_faster_than_radix_tree confirm engineering rationale
K02 (append-only no-dedup): TestPop multi-branch tests verify int→dict promotion path
K03 (chained hash invariant): test_chain_property_each_depends_on_previous + chain-break tests pin the property
K04 (max_cache_hit_length = N-1): test_max_cache_hit_length_is_n_minus_one verifies the logits-gap rule
K05 (lazy eviction): chain-break tests model the evict-then-find pattern
K06 (3-state lifecycle): TestTouchAndFree tests state transitions

k_heading_check.method: grep '^## ' prefix-cache.md showed K01-K10 with single-prefix headings (e.g., '## K01: Hash table...'); no double-prefix bug recurrence
